english: turn or move round (as vb. n.), *jiń-pa gyúr-te ltá-če* (jing pa gyur te lta ce) W. to look round, or back; *jiń-pa čag-če* (jing pa chag ce) W. to break one's neck; *jiń-pa zúm-če* (jing pa zum ce) W. to hug, to embrace; jiń-kyóg (jing kyog) a wry neck Cs.; jiń-khúń (jing khung) the nape of the neck Glr.; jiń-ltág (jing ltag) the back part of the neck Cs.

(Sch. also hjigs-pa ('jigs pa)) pf. bźibs (bzhibs) (gźibs (gzhibs)), fut. bźib (bzhib) (gźib (gzhib)), to suck, e.g. of a suckling baby; mchus (mchus) with the lips Lex.; khrag jibs-pa (khrag 'jib pa) to suck blood Lex.; to suck out, in, or up, to imbibe, absorb, also to blister, jib-mán ('jib sman) W. vesicatory.
1. Cs. a kind of sirup. — 2. Wdn. a medicinal herb.

B., C., a compound of earth and water, mud, clay, loam etc. (W. *ka-lag* (ka lag)); jím-skoń ('jim skong) a small cup of clay, a crucible Cs.; jím-gzugs ('jim gzugs) a figure formed of clay Glr.; rdó-rjei jím-pa (rdo rje'i 'jim pa) v. rdó-rje (rdo rje).
pf. bcil (bcil), fut. gźil (gzhil), to expel, eject, remove, turn off, phyir jíl-ba (phyir 'jil ba) Lex. id., e.g. noxious animals, vices etc.

I. vb. 1. pf. jus (jus), to seize, grasp, take hold of, c. dat., dprál-bai mdá-la jú-ba (dpral ba'i mda' la 'ju ba) grasping the arrow sticking in his forehead Glr.; gcig-la gcig jú-ba (gcig la gcig 'ju ba) taking firmly hold of each other (in a storm at sea) Glr.; to seize a person (in taking him prisoner) Pth.; lág-pa-nas (lag pa nas) to grasp by the hand, to shake hands (in greeting) Dzl. — 2. pf. bźus (bzhus), fut. bźu (bzhu), W. *źú-če* (zhu ce) *(or ju-če?)* ('ju ce) to melt, to digest, zas jú-ba (zas 'ju ba) to digest the food; ju slá-ba ('ju sla ba) digestible, ju dká-ba ('ju dka' ba) difficult of digestion; *ra jú-če* (ra 'ju ce) W. to digest intoxication, to sleep the fumes of wine away; ju-byéd ('ju byed) a sort of bile, the bile as the promoter of digestion Med. Cf. źú-ba (zhu ba) II. II. sbst. 1. digestion, jú-ba sla-o ('ju ba sla'o) the digestion is in order, is easy Med.; ju-stóbs chuń ('ju stobs chung) the digestive power is weak Med. — 2. a flea Sch. = ji-ba ('ji ba).
Cs. entrance, way of access, to a tank or river, Ghāt (Hind.).

I. pf. and imp. źugs (zhugs), W. *źúg-če* (zhug ce), vb. n., 1. to go or walk in, to enter, khań-pai (khang pa'i), or chui náń-du júg-pa (chu'i nang du 'jug pa) to go into the house, or into the water; rgyá-mtshor júg-pa (rgya mtshor 'jug pa) to put to sea, to set sail Dzl.; lám-du júg-pa (lam du 'jug pa) to set out, to start, to prosecute a journey; *mál-sa-la źúg-če* (mal sa la zhug ce) W. to go to bed. In a special sense: a. of a demon, entering into a man to take possession of him, hence *dé-źug-khan* (de zhug mkhan) W. possessed (by a demon); júg-sgo ('jug sgo) Med. the place where the demon entered the body. b. dgé-ba-la júg-pa (dge ba la 'jug pa) to walk in the path of virtue; acc. to Schr. júg-pa ('jug pa) by itself, without dgé-ba-la (dge ba la), implies the same, and in conformity with this a Lama gave the following explanation of the expression júg-pai las ('jug pa'i las) in Thgy.: works that are a consequence of having really entered upon the practice of virtue, positive good works, opp. to the negative good works of the ten virtues. chos-la júg-pa (chos la 'jug pa) to turn to religion, to be converted; chos (chos) or bstán-pa źíg-la júg-pa (bstan pa zhig la 'jug pa) to adopt a certain religion, a certain doctrine. c. bud-méd-la júg-pa (bud med la 'jug pa) to lie with, sleep with a woman Med.; *bár-la źúg-če* (bar la zhug ce) W. euph. expression for: to commit adultery. d. *mdún-du źúg-če* (mdun du zhug ce) W. to appear, in reference to gods. e. rjés-su júg-pa (rjes su 'jug pa) v. rjés-su (rjes su): — 2. to set or fall to, to begin, ríg-pa sbyáń-bas rtsóm-pa kún-la jug (rig pa sbyang bas rtsom pa kun la 'jug) a skilled, an experienced man is prepared for anything, knows how to set about it, how to manage it Med.; gen. with the inf.: to begin to do, to commence doing a thing, rtóg-pa-la (rtog pa la), resp. dgóńs-pa-la júg-pa (dgongs pa la 'jug pa) to begin to think upon Dzl., Glr.; stón-pa-la júg-pa (ston pa la 'jug pa) to begin to show Dzl.; gcig-la gcig rnám-par brlág-pa-la źúgs-pas (gcig la gcig rnam par brlag pa la zhugs pas) being in the best way of entirely exterminating one another Stg. — 3. pass. of júg-pa ('jug pa) II, 3, of letters: to be combined, to be preceded, to be followed, zla yíg sńón-du ba źúgs-čan (zla yig sngon du ba zhugs can) (words) having zl (zl) preceded by b (b), i.e. beginning with bzl (bzl) Zam. — 4.
